meaning: adv.潇洒地,漂亮地,火辣辣地,刺痛地
examples: The sergeant threw the frayed sheaf of football-pool coupons aside and snatched the radio. He saluted smartly, his huge belly juggling like a water gourd.
The woman was smartly dressed and well-spoken.
He dressed very smartly, which was important in those days.
Takal coaxes melodrama from first-person naturalism and smartly blends the allure of genre with do-it-yourself intimacy.
The people who took part were sometimes smartly dressed and at other times scruffy.
